subtypes.name: Positive_Count_Type
subtypes.code: subtype Positive_Count_Type is Count_Type range 1 .. Count_Type'Last;

subprograms.code: function "<" (Left : E.Sequence; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean
function "<=" (Left : E.Sequence; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean
function "<=" (Left : M.Set; Right : M.Set) return Boolean
function "<=" (Left : P.Map; Right : P.Map) return Boolean
function "=" (Left : E.Sequence; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean
function "=" (Left : M.Set; Right : M.Set) return Boolean
function "=" (Left : P.Map; Right : P.Map) return Boolean

subprograms.name: Contains
subprograms.code: function Contains (C : M.Set; K : Element_Type) return Boolean

documentation.description:
To improve readability of contracts, we rename the function used to
search for an element in the model to Contains.

subprograms.name: E_Elements_Equal
subprograms.code:
function E_Elements_Equal
  (Left : E.Sequence; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean

documentation.description: The elements of Left are "=" to the equivalent element in Right

subprograms.name: E_Elements_Included
subprograms.code:
function E_Elements_Included
  (Left : E.Sequence; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean

documentation.description: The elements of Left are contained in Right

subprograms.name: E_Elements_Included
subprograms.code:
function E_Elements_Included
  (Left : E.Sequence; Model : M.Set; Right : E.Sequence) return Boolean

documentation.description: The elements of Container contained in Model are in Right

subprograms.name: E_Elements_Included

documentation.description:
The elements of Container contained in Model are in Left and others
are in Right.

subprograms.name: Element_Logic_Equal
subprograms.code: function Element_Logic_Equal (Left, Right : Element_Type) return Boolean

subprograms.name: Elements
subprograms.code: function Elements (Container : Set) return E.Sequence

documentation.returns:
The Elements sequence represents the underlying list structure of
sets that is used for iteration. It stores the actual values of
elements in the set. It does not model cursors.

It only contains keys contained in Model

It contains all the elements contained in Model

It has no duplicate

subprograms.name: Find
subprograms.code:
function Find
  (Container : E.Sequence; Item : Element_Type) return Count_Type

subprograms.name: Lift_Abstraction_Level
subprograms.code: procedure Lift_Abstraction_Level (Container : Set)

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description:
Lift_Abstraction_Level is a ghost procedure that does nothing but
assume that we can access the same elements by iterating over
positions or cursors.
This information is not generally useful except when switching from
a low-level, cursor-aware view of a container, to a high-level,
position-based view.

subprograms.name: Mapping_Preserved

documentation.description:
Right contains all the cursors of Left
Right contains all the elements of Left
Mappings from cursors to elements induced by E_Left, P_Left
and E_Right, P_Right are the same.

subprograms.name: Mapping_Preserved_Except

documentation.description:
Right contains all the cursors of Left
Mappings from cursors to elements induced by E_Left, P_Left
and E_Right, P_Right are the same except for Position.

subprograms.name: Model
subprograms.code: function Model (Container : Set) return M.Set

documentation.returns:
The high-level model of a set is a set of elements. Neither cursors
nor order of elements are represented in this model. Elements are
modeled up to equivalence.

subprograms.name: Positions
subprograms.code: function Positions (Container : Set) return P.Map

documentation.description:
Positions of cursors are smaller than the container's length
No two cursors have the same position. Note that we do not
state that there is a cursor in the map for each position,
as it is rarely needed.
documentation.returns:
The Positions map is used to model cursors. It only contains valid
cursors and maps them to their position in the container.

generic_instantiations.name: E
generic_instantiations.code:
package E is new
  SPARK.Containers.Functional.Vectors
    (Element_Type                   => Element_Type,
     Index_Type                     => Positive_Count_Type,
     "="                            => "=",
     Eq_Reflexive                   => Eq_Checks.Eq_Reflexive,
     Eq_Symmetric                   => Eq_Checks.Eq_Symmetric,
     Eq_Transitive                  => Eq_Checks.Eq_Transitive,
     Equivalent_Elements            => "=",
     Equivalent_Elements_Reflexive  => Lift_Eq.Eq_Reflexive,
     Equivalent_Elements_Symmetric  => Eq_Checks.Eq_Symmetric,
     Equivalent_Elements_Transitive => Eq_Checks.Eq_Transitive);

generic_instantiations.name: Eq_Checks
generic_instantiations.code:
package Eq_Checks is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Equivalence_Checks
    (T                   => Element_Type,
     Eq                  => "=",
     Param_Eq_Reflexive  => Eq_Reflexive,
     Param_Eq_Symmetric  => Eq_Symmetric,
     Param_Eq_Transitive => Eq_Transitive);

documentation.description: Check that the actual parameter for "=" is an equivalence relation

generic_instantiations.name: Eq_Elements_Checks
generic_instantiations.code:
package Eq_Elements_Checks is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Equivalence_Checks_Eq
    (T                     => Element_Type,
     Eq                    => Equivalent_Elements,
     "="                   => "=",
     Param_Equal_Reflexive => Eq_Checks.Eq_Reflexive,
     Param_Eq_Reflexive    => Equivalent_Elements_Reflexive,
     Param_Eq_Symmetric    => Equivalent_Elements_Symmetric,
     Param_Eq_Transitive   => Equivalent_Elements_Transitive);

documentation.description:
Check that the actual parameter for Equivalent_Elements is an
equivalence relation and that it is compatible with "=".

generic_instantiations.name: Hash_Checks
generic_instantiations.code:
package Hash_Checks is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Hash_Equivalence_Checks
    (T                     => Element_Type,
     "="                   => Equivalent_Elements,
     Hash                  => Hash,
     Param_Hash_Equivalent => Hash_Equivalent);

documentation.description:
Check that the actual parameter for Hash returns the same value for
all equivalent elements.

generic_instantiations.name: Lift_Eq
generic_instantiations.code:
package Lift_Eq is new
  SPARK.Containers.Parameter_Checks.Lift_Eq_Reflexive
    (T                  => Element_Type,
     "="                => "=",
     Eq                 => "=",
     Param_Eq_Reflexive => Eq_Checks.Eq_Reflexive);

generic_instantiations.name: M
generic_instantiations.code:
package M is new
  SPARK.Containers.Functional.Sets
    (Element_Type                   => Element_Type,
     Equivalent_Elements            => Equivalent_Elements,
     Equivalent_Elements_Reflexive  => Eq_Elements_Checks.Eq_Reflexive,
     Equivalent_Elements_Symmetric  => Eq_Elements_Checks.Eq_Symmetric,
     Equivalent_Elements_Transitive => Eq_Elements_Checks.Eq_Transitive);

generic_instantiations.name: P
generic_instantiations.code:
package P is new
  SPARK.Containers.Functional.Maps
    (Key_Type                       => Cursor,
     Element_Type                   => Positive_Count_Type,
     Equivalent_Keys                => "=",
     Enable_Handling_Of_Equivalence => False);
